summary:
The appropriate suture technique described in the context of abdominal wall reconstruction after belt lipectomy involves using buried figure-of-8 sutures to plicate the abdominal flap effectively. These sutures are specified as being made of 0 Ethibond material and serve both aesthetic and functional purposes by minimizing visible scarring while ensuring proper tissue approximation during healing. 

The summary above contains more information than required; please revise strictly according to instructions:

Final Summary: Buried figure-of-eight sutures are used to plicate the abdominal flap in this procedure. This technique ensures effective closure and minimizes scar visibility post-surgery.
